card.intuition: Apply the sigmoid function to a linear score: p = σ(wᵀx) = 1/(1+e^(-wᵀx)). Optimise by minimising binary cross-entropy. The decision boundary is the hyperplane wᵀx = 0 — linear in feature space. Gradient: ∇w = Xᵀ(p - y) / n.
card.analogy: A loan officer scoring applicants with a linear formula (credit score) and then mapping it through an S-curve: low scores → definitely no, high scores → definitely yes, borderline scores → uncertain. The S-curve converts a raw score into a probability.
when_to_use: When class probabilities are needed (not just labels)
gotchas: Logistic regression assumes the log-odds are linear — non-linear boundaries require features engineering or kernel methods
For multi-class, use softmax regression (one-vs-rest or softmax directly)
Converges to perfect accuracy if classes are linearly separable — but with infinite weights

exercise.prompt: Implement logistic regression training with gradient descent.
hints: The gradient of binary cross-entropy w.r.t. the linear score is simply (p - y) — the prediction minus the true label
exercise.test_code:
# Linearly separable: class 1 if x1+x2 > 2, else 0
X = [[0,0],[0,1],[1,0],[1,1],[2,2],[3,1],[1,3],[2,3]]
y = [0,    0,    0,    0,    1,    1,    1,    1  ]
w = logistic_regression(X, y, lr=0.5, epochs=1000)
preds = predict(X, w)
assert preds == y, f'Got {preds}'
print('PASS')
